rationale: This signal set is heavily weighted toward general public interest (Google autocomplete, Bluesky lifestyle posts, YouTube beginner guides) rather than breeder-specific or genetics-focused debate. Most morphs mentioned (cappuccino, lilly white, axanthic) already have published slugs. The few YouTube signals that hint at depth—"Don't buy super cappuccino it's a scam," "Why so much hate for cappuccino geckos"—suggest community friction, but without forum threads or Reddit posts showing the actual disagreement, it's hard to ground a myth-busting or genetics piece. The queued slug "cappuccino-breeding-economics" also pre-empts the most promising angle. Autocomplete volume on "crested gecko morph calculator" and "morph chart" hints at demand for breeding/prediction tools, but that's infrastructure, not editorial. No signals break through to justify a full post.
